psalm-114-1.verse:
When Israel went forth from Egypt,
  The house of Jacob from a people of strange language,
psalm-114-1.prayer: Lord, thank You for leading Your people out of bondage. Lead me out of anything that keeps me from walking freely with You.

psalm-114-2.verse:
Judah became His sanctuary,
  Israel, His dominion.
psalm-114-2.prayer: Father, make my life a place where Your presence is honored and Your rule is welcomed.

psalm-114-3.verse:
The sea looked and fled;
  The Jordan turned back.
psalm-114-3.prayer: Lord, every obstacle moves before Your power. Help me trust You when the way ahead seems impossible.

psalm-114-4.verse:
The mountains skipped like rams,
  The hills, like lambs.
psalm-114-4.prayer: Father, all creation responds to Your presence. Teach me to live with reverence, joy, and awe before You.

psalm-114-5.verse:
What ails you, O sea, that you flee?
  O Jordan, that you turn back?
psalm-114-5.prayer: Lord, when mountains or seas stand before me, remind me that nothing can resist Your command.

psalm-114-6.verse:
O mountains, that you skip like rams?
  O hills, like lambs?
psalm-114-6.prayer: Father, awaken my heart to Your greatness. Let every stronghold tremble before Your holy presence.

psalm-114-7.verse:
Tremble, O earth, before the Lord,
  Before the God of Jacob,
psalm-114-7.prayer: Lord, help me tremble before You with holy reverence. Let my life honor Your majesty and power.

psalm-114-8.verse:
Who turned the rock into a pool of water,
  The flint into a fountain of water.
psalm-114-8.prayer: Father, thank You for bringing water from the rock. Provide refreshment, life, and hope in every dry place.

psalm-114-closing.prayer:
Lord, thank You for Your mighty deliverance, holy presence, and life-giving provision. Lead me out of bondage, move every obstacle before me, and refresh the dry places of my soul. Help me live with reverence, joy, and trust as I follow Your power and presence.

In Jesus’ name, Amen.
